content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 3, 1994

Bradley Sullivan
Sutter Creek City Attorney
18 Main Street
P.O. Box 366
Sutter Creek, CA  95685

Re:  Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-94-175

Dear Mr. Sullivan:

This is in response to your letter requesting advice on
behalf of Sutter Creek City Councilmember Edward Swift regarding
his responsibilities under the conflict-of-interest provisions of
the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

Please note that nothing in this letter should be construed
to evaluate any conduct which may have already taken place.  In
addition, please note that other laws may apply to your facts.
Commission advice is limited to the provisions of the Political
Reform Act.

QUESTION

If Councilmember Swift has a conflict of interest with
respect to a decision regarding the development of property
adjacent to his own, does the councilmember have to resign from
office?

_______________

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000-18954.  All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento, CA  95804-0807  •  (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-94-175
Page 2


CONCLUSION

The Act does not require the councilmember's resignation.
However, Councilmember Swift is prohibited from making,
participating in making, or otherwise using his official position
to influence the governmental decision if the decision will have a
reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on his economic
interests.

FACTS

The Sutter Creek City Council has been considering the
development of property in Sutter Creek for a new shopping center.
Recently, the anchor tenant for the shopping center has indicated
that it may be reconsidering its decision to locate in the new
shopping center.

In response, the developer has been in discussions with a
local automobile dealer regarding the sale of the shopping center
site. The city anticipates that if the sale occurs, the city will
be asked by the developer or the new owner of the automobile
dealership to waive some fees and to rebate some of the sales tax
receipts to the automobile dealer.

Councilmember Swift owns property adjacent to the site. In
addition, approximately three years ago, Councilmember Swift sold
property to the original developer to be used in the development.
You have asked whether the councilmember is required to abstain
from the decisions concerning the automobile dealership or resign
from the city council.

ANALYSIS

The Act was adopted by the voters of California by initiative
in 1974. The purpose for the conflict-of-interest provision of
the Act was to ensure that public officials, whether elected or
appointed, would perform their duties in an impartial manner, free
from bias caused by their own financial interests or the financial
interests of persons who have supported them. (Section 81001(b).)

In furtherance of this goal, Section 87100 of the Act
prohibits any public official from making, participating in
making, or otherwise using his or her official position to
influence a governmental decision in which the official has a
financial interest.

Section 87103 specifies that a public official has a
financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable
that the decision will have a material financial effect,

Our File No. A-94-175
Page 3


distinguishable from the effect on the public generally, on the
official or a member of his or her immediate family or on:

     (b) Any real property in which the public
official has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars-($1,000) or more.

     (c) Any source of income, other than gifts and
other than loans by a commercial lending institution in the regular course of business on terms
available to the public without regard to official
status, aggregating two hundred fifty dollars
($250) or more in value provided to, received by or
promised to the public official within 12 months
prior to the time when the decision is made.

     You stated that Councilmember Swift owned real property in
proximity to the development in question. His real property
interests would be considered potentially disqualifying economic
interests under Section 87103.

     In addition, Mr. Swift previously sold property to the
developer. If he received more than $250 from the developer
during the 12 months preceding a decision (even if the sale
occurred three years earlier), the developer would be a source of
income for the purposes of Section 87103.2

     You stated in your letter that the councilmember's real
property will be materially affected by the decision pursuant to
the standards of Regulation 18702.3(a)(1).3 You have asked,
assuming that the councilmember has a conflict of interest,
whether the Act requires the councilmember's resignation.

     As stated above, Section 87100 prohibits a public official
from making, participating in making, or otherwise using his or
her official position to influence a governmental decision in
which the official has a financial interest. Thus, the conflict-
of-interest provisions of the Act do not require that an official
step down from office, but that the official does not participate


_____________________________

2    For purposes of the remainder of the analysis, we are assuming
that the councilmember did not receive $250 in income from any
participants in the proceeding within the past 12 months.

3    Of course as noted above, even if the real property were not
materially affected, the councilmember would have a conflict of
interest in the decision if he received $250 from the original
developer within the past 12 months, and the developer is
materially affected by the decision. (See, Regulation
18702.1(a)(1) and (b); Regulation 18702.2.)

Our File No. A-94-175
Page 4


in or influence a decision that will have a financial effect on
the official's own economic interests.

     If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.⁴

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Steven G. Churchwell
                                        General Counsel

                                   By: John W. Wallace
                                       Counsel, Legal Division




_________________________
     ⁴ Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.  Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.

The Act does not require the councilmember’s resignation.
However,
Councilmember Swift is prohibited from making,
participating in making, or otherwise using his official position
to influence the govermnpta1 decision if the decision will have a
reasonably foreseeable material financial effect on his economic
interests.
